sections.heading: The Commission should draw up a report in respect of the delegation of power not later than nine months
sections.title: Commission report on delegation of power extension
sections.summary: The Commission must prepare a report on the delegation of power within nine months before the end of a five-year period. The delegation can be extended for identical durations unless the European Parliament or Council opposes the extension at least three months before each extension deadline. This section establishes a timeline for reporting and conditions for extending delegated powers without opposition from key legislative bodies. The Commission must prepare a report on delegated powers within nine months. This obligation applies to the European Commission and is part of the regulatory framework for the GDPR. The report aims to ensure transparency and accountability in the delegation process for data protection measures.

sections.title: Regulation adoption timeline for cybersecurity rules
sections.summary: This Regulation establishes cybersecurity requirements for digital products and mandates market surveillance. It applies to products with digital elements that connect to devices or networks. Economic operators must comply with cybersecurity design, development, production, and vulnerability handling obligations. The Regulation also includes rules for market surveillance and enforcement.

requirements.text: This Regulation establishes cybersecurity requirements for digital products
Economic operators must comply with cybersecurity design, development, and production obligations
Economic operators must comply with vulnerability handling process obligations
Market surveillance rules must be enforced

sections.source_quote: HAVE ADOPTED THIS REGULATION: CHAPTER I GENERAL PROVISIONS Article 1 Subject matter This Regulation lays down: (a) rules for the making available on the market of products with digital elements to ensure the cybersecurity of such products; (b) essential cybersecurity requirements for the design, development and production of products with digital elements, and obligations for economic operators in relation to those products with respect to cybersecurity; (c) essential cybersecurity requirements for the vulnerability handling processes put in place by manufacturers to ensure the cybersecurity of products with digital elements during the time the products are expected to be in use, and obligations for economic operators in relation to those processes; (d) rules on market surveillance, including monitoring, and enforcement of the rules and requirements referred to in this Article.

sections.title: CE marking and conformity declaration for digital products
sections.summary: Manufacturers must affix the CE marking with the notified body's identification number to each digital product meeting Part I of Annex I requirements. They must also create a written declaration of conformity for each product model, retaining it for 10 years after market placement or support period, whichever is longer. The declaration must identify the product model and be accessible to authorities upon request.

sections.title: CE marking and conformity declaration for digital products
sections.summary: Manufacturers must affix the CE marking with the notified body's identification number to each digital product meeting Part I of Annex I requirements. sections.title: Reporting EU-type certificates to authorities and other notified bodies
sections.summary: Each notified body must inform its notifying authorities about issued or withdrawn EU-type examination certificates and any additions. They must also share information on refused, suspended, or restricted certificates with other notified bodies. Authorities can request copies of certificates and technical documentation, and notified bodies must retain copies until certificate expiry.

sections.source_quote: Conformity based on full quality assurance is the conformity assessment procedure whereby the manufacturer fulfils the obligations set out in points 2 and 5 of this Part, and ensures and declares on its sole responsibility that the products with digital elements or product categories concerned satisfy the essential cybersecurity requirements set out in Part I of Annex I and that the vulnerability handling processes put in place by the manufacturer meet the requirements set out in Part II of Annex I.

sections.title: Reporting EU-type certificates to authorities and other notified bodies
